figures.caption: Figure 8: Demonstration of dynamic, reactive and precise manipulation examples with XArm7 and TeleDex: (a) dynamic object catching, (b) reactive goalkeeping, (c) precise buzz-wire loop tracing.
Figure 9: Simulated fruit pick-and-place task in MuJoCo used for policy training experiment.
Figure 10: Compilation of TeleDex deployments across multiple robot platforms in both our experiments and third-party settings.

Figure 2: TeleDex System Architecture. The system supports two settings: (left) a Dexterous Setting using a 3D-printable wrist-mounted interface for 21-DoF hand and 6-DoF wrist tracking, and (right) a Phone-Only Setting for 6-DoF endeffector control. Captured states are processed via Dex-Retarget to map inputs to arbitrary robotic URDF models.
Figure 5: MolmoSpaces-Bench user study setup: left, using SpaceMouse; right, using a keyboard.
Figure 3: Screenshots of TeleDex phone application.
Figure 6: Average time-to-success for the MS-Open and MSPick tasks, across users in MolmoSpaces-Bench.

Figure 7: Dexterous tasks used for user study of RQ2: (a) pick-and-place with objects of varying geometries. (b) targeted piano key pressing. (c) egg container opening and object extraction. (d) participant performing tasks during the user study.
